text.title: django-coding-standards Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-coding-standards as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Bare except: clause on Model.objects.get() calls.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Catches every exception including KeyboardInterrupt and SystemExit. Always catch the specific exception: Model.DoesNotExist. This lets actual errors (OOM, corrupt DB) propagate and fail the task correctly.

failure-mode.description: Validation in Model.clean() for DRF API flows.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: clean() is called by ModelForms and Django admin, not by DRF serializers. Validation in clean() is silently skipped on API requests, leading to invalid data in the database.

failure-mode.description: Serializer with password in Meta.fields without write_only: True.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Password hash leaks into API responses. Always add extra_kwargs = {'password': {'write_only': True}} so the field is accepted on write but never returned on read.
